name: Peanut Buttery Stir-Fry
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the rice until tender.
2. Dice the tofu and heat 1 teaspoon oil in a wok or frying pan (skillet). Stir-fry the tofu until lightly browned, then remove from the wok.
3. Slice the onion thinly and slice the carrot into matchsticks. Chop the beans finely. Heat 2 teaspoons oil in the wok and stir-fry these ingredients for 2-3 minutes.
4. Chop the cabbage leaves and add them to the wok along with the beansprouts. Continue stir-frying until just tender.
5. Crush the garlic, grate the ginger finely, and heat the remaining teaspoon oil in a small saucepan. Add the garlic and ginger and cook for a minute or two. Stir in the peanut butter and water until smooth. (This step can be done before the vegetables start cooking; the rest should wait until they are nearly ready.)
6. Add the lemon juice, soy sauce, and soymilk to the saucepan and stir well.
7. Return the tofu to the wok and stir in the peanut butter sauce. Mix well and serve over the rice.
